name: Whole Wheat Refrigerator Roll Dough
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon Lite salt or less or none
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare dough for refrigeration, dissolve yeast in warm water.
2. Combine with dried potatoes and brown sugar.
3. Add lite salt, oil, and egg white. Stir in 1 cup white flour and 2 cups whole wheat flour.
4. Mix in enough additional white flour (1/2 to 1 cup) to make the dough easy to handle.
5. Knead on a floured board until the dough is smooth and elastic.
6. Place in a lightly oiled bowl (large enough for dough to double). Cover tightly and refrigerate at least 8 hours, not more than five days.
7. Two Hours Before Serving:
a. Remove from refrigerator.
b. Shape into rolls and allow to rise 30-45 minutes or until doubled in size.
c. Bake in a 350°F oven for 20-25 minutes until lightly browned. Makes 18 rolls.
Other Uses:
- For Calzones: Remove dough from refrigerator and roll out in circles. For filling, see Calzones.
- For Pizza Crust: Divide into half. Pat onto two lightly oiled baking sheets. Let rise 15 minutes. Cover with pizza sauce, vegetables, and grated low-fat cheese, then bake in a 350°F oven for 25 minutes. Makes two crusts.
